scores.correctness_notes: The agent covers some correct high-level concepts (events exist, when to request certs) but answers about V4 API while the gold standard is about the V1 interface spec. Misses key gold standard points: CSRs/CA certificates, revocation triggering new requests, and the specific error codes (ip_not_allowed 101, domain_not_allowed 102, etc.).
scores.hallucination_notes: Multiple hallucinated items: 'certificate_denied' event (gold standard mentions certificate_available, certificate_expiring, certificate_invalidated); 'CertificateError' class with .code/.name/.message/.reason/.provider/.endpoint attributes; 'renewal_relative_time' parameter; 'refresh_events' parameter; '.renew_certificate()' method on ProviderCertificate; '.chain_as_pem()' method; 'CertificateRequestAttributes' class. The entire V4 API framing (TLSCertificatesRequiresV4, TLSCertificatesProvidesV4) appears to be a fabricated version not referenced in the gold standard.
